key_points: Light is a form of energy that enables us to see objects
Reflection of light occurs when light bounces back after striking a surface
The laws of reflection state that the angle of incidence equals the angle of reflection and the incident ray, reflected ray and normal lie in the same plane
Spherical mirrors are of two types: concave mirrors and convex mirrors
Refraction is the bending of light when it passes from one medium to another due to change in speed
examples: Plane mirror forming an erect and virtual image
Concave mirrors used in shaving mirrors and headlights
Convex mirrors used as rear-view mirrors in vehicles
A pencil appearing bent when placed in a glass of water due to refraction
formulas: Snell’s Law → sin i / sin r = constant
exam_tips: Remember the laws of reflection and their ray diagrams
Practice ray diagrams for concave and convex mirrors
Understand the mirror formula and magnification relation
Know the concept of refraction and Snell’s law with examples
